Supports sorting by 'name' (default), 'createdAt', 'channelType', or 'description' in ascending or descending order.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of accounts associated with a specific identity.

get.description: Searches for accounts by identifier match. If identifier parameter is provided, returns accounts whose claim values contain the identifier (case-insensitive). If no identifier is provided, returns all accounts with their identifiers from active claim sets marked as identifiers. Use identifierType to filter by EMAIL, PHONE, or ALL (default).

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Optional partial identifier to search for. If not provided, returns all accounts with identifiers

schema.description: Optional partial identifier to search for. If not provided, returns all accounts with identifiers

parameters.name: identifierType
parameters.description: Type of identifier to filter by: ALL (default), EMAIL, or PHONE

schema.description: Type of identifier to filter by: ALL (default), EMAIL, or PHONE

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number (1-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of results per page

parameters.name: X-ORIGINAL-HOST
parameters.description: Original Host Header for multi-tenant identification

200.description: Search results found (may be empty if no matches)

delete.description: Deletes a filter based on it's id.

parameters.description: Original Host Header for multi-tenant identification

delete.description: Revokes all OAuth2 authorizations for a specific registered client

CreateTypeRequest.description: Request to create a new schema type

code.description: Unique type code (letters, numbers, dashes, underscores)

itemsTypeId.description: Items type ID (required when kind=ARRAY). References another schema type.

constraints.description: Optional constraints. For SCALAR: PATTERN, MIN_LENGTH, etc. For ARRAY: MIN_ITEMS, MAX_ITEMS, UNIQUE_ITEMS
Constraints map keyed by constraint type name

itemsTypeId.description: Items type ID for ARRAY types

itemsTypeCode.description: Items type code for ARRAY types (convenience)

constraints.description: Constraints map keyed by constraint type (camelCase)

cacheValid.description: Indicates whether cached JSON schema exists

isBuiltin.description: Indicates if this is a built-in scalar type

TargetTypeRef.description: Lightweight reference to a schema type

isBuiltin.description: Whether this is a built-in type

order.description: Explicit field order (optional, defaults to append)

CheckRoleAccountsRequest.description: Request to check which accounts from a given list have a specific role assigned

accountIds.description: List of account IDs to check against the role

CheckRoleAccountsResponse.description: Response containing the account IDs from the input list that have the specified role assigned

roleId.description: Unique identifier of the role that was checked

matchingAccountIds.description: List of account IDs from the request that have this role assigned. Only includes active accounts. If an account ID from the request is not in this list, it means the account either doesn't have the role or is inactive.

channelId.description: Unique identifier of the channel this factor belongs to

TotpEnrollByAccountRequest.description: Request to enroll TOTP using account name

accountName.description: The account name to identify or create the account

accountType.description: The type of account. Defaults to CUSTOMER if not provided.

identityProviderType.description: The subtype of account. Defaults to LOCAL if not provided.

accountId.description: The account ID (existing or newly created)

identityId.description: The identity ID associated with the account

factorId.description: The unique identifier of the TOTP factor

seed.description: Base32 encoded secret seed for TOTP generation. Use this to configure an authenticator app.

accountCreated.description: Whether the account was newly created during this enrollment

TotpValidateByAccountRequest.description: Request to validate TOTP using account name

accountType.description: The type of account. Defaults to CUSTOMER if not provided.

identityProviderType.description: The subtype of account. Defaults to LOCAL if not provided.

accountId.description: The account ID resolved from accountName

totpValidated.description: Whether the TOTP was validated successfully

ClaimValueRequest.description: claim value structure with simplified format

value.description: The claim value - can be a string or array of strings

isIdentifier.description: Whether this claim value should be used as an identifier for the account

primaryIndex.description: For array values, the index of the primary value (0-based)

CreateClaimValueRequest.description: request to create claim values with simplified structure

ownerId.description: Unique identifier of the owner these claim values belong to (account or identity)

ownerType.description: Type of owner for these claims

businessId.description: Unique identifier of the business this account belongs to

accountType.description: Account type/schema scope for these claims

identityProviderType.description: Optional subtype scope by identity provider type.

claims.description: Map of claim names to their values. The key is the claim name (e.g., 'employee_id', 'department'), and the value contains the actual claim value along with optional metadata like isIdentifier and primaryIndex.

ClaimValueResponse.description: claim value information response with simplified structure

id.description: Unique identifier of the claim value

ownerType.description: Type of owner for this claim value

ownerId.description: Unique identifier of the owner (account or identity) this claim value belongs to

schemaSetId.description: Unique identifier of the schema set

value.description: The full claim value as JSON. Can be a scalar value or complex object/array.

primaryIndex.description: For array values, the index of the primary value (0-based)

CreateClaimSchemaSetRequest.description: Create request for claim schema set (business/accountType → list of schema types)

schemas.description: Schemas included in this set, with optional order/required flags

SchemaRef.description: Reference to a schema type inside the set

identityProviderType.description: Optional subtype scope by identity provider type.

description.example: Schemas required to identify workforce users
